section: Class 10 Economics - Chapter 3: Money and Credit

questions.question: What is the main problem of the barter system?
questions.explanation: In barter system, both parties must want each other's goods, which makes exchange difficult.

questions.question: Name one function of money.
questions.explanation: Money acts as a medium of exchange and removes the need for double coincidence of wants.

questions.question: What are demand deposits?
questions.correct_answer: Deposits that can be withdrawn anytime
questions.explanation: Demand deposits are bank deposits that can be withdrawn on demand and used through cheques.

questions.question: Who issues currency notes in India?
questions.explanation: The RBI is the central bank of India and has the authority to issue currency notes.

questions.question: What is credit?
questions.correct_answer: Agreement to borrow money with promise of repayment
questions.explanation: Credit refers to borrowing money with the obligation to repay it along with interest.

questions.question: What is collateral?
questions.correct_answer: Asset pledged as security for a loan
questions.explanation: Collateral is an asset such as land or property used as security against a loan.

questions.question: Name one formal source of credit.
questions.explanation: Banks are formal sources of credit regulated by the RBI.

questions.question: Name one informal source of credit.
questions.explanation: Moneylenders are informal sources of credit and often charge high interest rates.

questions.question: What is a debt trap?
questions.correct_answer: Borrowing new loans to repay old ones
questions.explanation: A debt trap occurs when a borrower keeps taking loans to repay previous loans due to high interest burden.

questions.question: What is the role of RBI in credit?
questions.explanation: RBI regulates banks and ensures they follow rules while providing loans.

notes.title: Barter vs Money
notes.content: Barter required double coincidence of wants, while money acts as a medium of exchange and simplifies trade.

notes.title: Functions of Money
notes.content: Money acts as a medium of exchange, unit of account and store of value.

notes.title: Formal and Informal Credit
notes.content: Formal credit is provided by banks and cooperatives under RBI supervision, while informal credit comes from moneylenders and traders.

notes.title: Terms of Credit
notes.content: Important terms of credit include interest rate, collateral, documentation and repayment period.

notes.title: Debt Trap
notes.content: High interest rates and repeated borrowing can push borrowers into a debt trap.

facts.fact: Currency notes and coins are considered legal tender in India.
Banks use deposits to provide loans and earn profit through interest.
Formal credit is generally safer than informal credit.
Self Help Groups (SHGs) help poor households access credit.
Interest is the cost paid by the borrower for using borrowed money.
